title: Path fact: Camera work centers on the Camera singleton, typed transform structs, and static data layout
text:
Camera work centers on the Camera singleton, typed transform structs, and static data layout
cm
Camera code usually becomes reviewable when raw globals and offsets are mapped to Camera, CmSubject, CameraTransformState, CameraBounds, CameraDebugMode, and camera.static.h data instead of inventing local storage or leaving opaque offsets.
src/melee/cm/** include/melee/cm/**
The target path is under src/melee/cm or touches camera callbacks from db/vi/gm. Generated output accesses camera globals, CameraBox/CmSubject lists, pause camera fields, or HSD_CObj state.
Use cm/types.h for Camera, CmSubject, CameraTransformState, CameraBounds, CameraDebugMode, and CameraModeCallbacks before adding temporary fields. Check camera.static.h and nearby accessors when matching singleton data such as cm_80452C68 or static descriptors. Keep camera.h prototypes concrete when the body proves argument or return types.
Invent camera globals to force data order when camera.static.h or symbols metadata owns the layout. Treat db or vi camera callbacks as independent of cm state when they call camera APIs. Leave accessor bodies as raw pointer copies when cm/types.h exposes the field.

title: Path fact: Debug code is split into thematic modules with typed dev-state, camera, item, and OSContext helpers
text:
Debug code is split into thematic modules with typed dev-state, camera, item, and OSContext helpers
db
Debug files now prefer module-specific db*.c surfaces and db.h prototypes over the old monolithic db_2253 assumptions. Reuse typed debug structs and real Dolphin/OSContext layouts before adding local externs or scratch declarations.
src/melee/db/** include/melee/db/**
The target path is under src/melee/db. Generated output touches debug camera, dev menu, item/debug state, OSContext, or FPU save/load code.
Check db.h and the specific db*.c module before recreating declarations from generated output. Use Dolphin OSContext/FPU layout evidence where debug code saves or displays CPU state. Preserve split and symbol metadata when old db_2253-era names are moved into module-specific files.
Assume a debug helper still belongs to the former monolithic db_2253 file. Add local externs for debug globals without checking db.h and module statics. Treat debug camera code as separate from cm camera state when calls flow through camera APIs.

title: Path fact: Effect targets hinge on gfx-id dispatch, EF_Effect updates, and attach/generator helper families
text:
Effect targets hinge on gfx-id dispatch, EF_Effect updates, and attach/generator helper families
ef
Effect code is usually clearer when efSync/efAsync dispatch ranges, EF_Effect fields, attach/generator helper families, facing-dir helpers, and update callbacks are used directly instead of raw vararg or void-pointer scaffolding.
src/melee/ef/** include/melee/ef/**
The target path is under src/melee/ef. Generated output branches by gfx id, walks EF_Effect chains, or passes variadic attach/generator arguments.
Use ef/forward.h and ef/types.h to name EF_Effect, EF_QueuedEffect, EF_EffectDesc, and EF_UpdateFn. For efSync_Spawn and efalt dispatch, check gfx-id ranges and helper families before preserving large generated switch/goto output. Keep update callbacks on EF_Effect records explicit when source shows SetRot, SetScale, transition, or facing-dir behavior.
Treat every vararg slot as an anonymous void* when helper names reveal Vec3, HSD_JObj, or scale/facing data. Rewrite attach/generator dispatch without checking efSync_Spawn range comments and efalt sibling cases. Assume effect load-kind globals are incidental; they drive sync vs async dispatch.

title: Path fact: Use Fighter_GObj, GET_FIGHTER, motion-var union arms, and action-specific headers
text:
Use Fighter_GObj, GET_FIGHTER, motion-var union arms, and action-specific headers
ft
Fighter code commonly wants Fighter_GObj*, GET_FIGHTER(gobj), concrete ftCommon_MotionVars or character-specific var arms, typed Fighter bitfields, action-specific ftCo_* headers, and truthful prototypes. Split history matters: many common action files moved out of monolithic ft ranges.
src/melee/ft/** include/melee/ft/**
The target path is under src/melee/ft or a caller/callee is fighter-owned. Generated output accesses gobj->user_data, x222* flags, motion vars, ft_data, common action states, item pickup fields, or per-character attributes.
Use Fighter_GObj* and GET_FIGHTER when local/header evidence shows fighter user data. Check ft/types.h, ftCommon motion-variable structs, and character types before using M2C_FIELD or raw flag bytes. Prefer action-specific headers in src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon and per-character folders before adding declarations. When copied or common action files were split, check config/symbol ownership and sibling action files for prototype/data shapes.
Borrow unrelated FighterVars or motion-var fields because m2c guessed them. Leave raw x222* masks or M2C_FIELD when typed bitfields already exist. Patch common fighter code without checking affected item, ground, player, and library headers touched by the same type.

title: Path fact: Game-mode work depends on MinorScene tables, static headers, and typed setup/result structs
text:
Game-mode work depends on MinorScene tables, static headers, and typed setup/result structs
gm
Game-mode decomp often improves when scene/minor callback tables, static data headers, split ownership, and typed StartMeleeData/MatchPlayerData/result/tournament structs are recovered together. Header cleanup in gm callers is usually part of the match.
src/melee/gm/** include/melee/gm/**
The target path is under src/melee/gm or a menu/video caller uses gm scene data. Generated output touches MinorScene, MajorScene, StartMeleeData, MatchPlayerData, result/player stats, tournament data, or scene OnLoad/OnEnter/OnLeave callbacks.
Check gm/forward.h, gm/types.h, module headers, and *.static.h before adding local statics or externs. Use MinorScene/MajorScene table evidence and OnLoad/OnEnter/OnLeave naming when the source path is a scene setup unit. When arrays such as kills[4], controller maps, or result/player fields are evidenced, prefer arrays/typed structs over scalar offset fields.
Invent scene callback declarations locally when gm headers own them. Move data or static BSS order without checking symbols and split metadata. Treat menu, vi, or ty callers as unrelated when gm scene tables reference them.

title: Path fact: Stage targets revolve around StageData, StageCallbacks, Ground gv unions, and file-local data tables
text:
Stage targets revolve around StageData, StageCallbacks, Ground gv unions, and file-local data tables
gr
Stage code usually wants Ground_GObj/Ground*, GET_GROUND, the correct Ground::gv union arm, StageData/StageCallbacks tables, and stage-specific static data instead of raw mapData offsets or unrelated gv fields.
src/melee/gr/** include/melee/gr/**
The target path is under src/melee/gr. Generated output accesses gobj->user_data, Ground gv fields, StageCallbacks, stage archive/model data, material callbacks, map joints, or route/object tables.
Use gr/types.h and the stage's header/static data before naming Ground gv fields. Check StageCallbacks and StageData tables near the top of the stage file before implementing spawn/setup functions. Preserve data/table ownership and symbols when callback tables, route data, material data, or stage parameters drive matching.
Use a different stage's GroundVars arm because generated output guessed it. Add duplicate mapData layout knowledge when Ground or gr/types.h owns the field. Treat item/stage interactions as one-sided; gr files often call item helpers with Item_GObj and ItemVars.

title: Path fact: Interface files use typed HUD/status state, HSD_GObj lifetimes, text helpers, and game-mode callsites
text:
Interface files use typed HUD/status state, HSD_GObj lifetimes, text helpers, and game-mode callsites
if
Interface decomp is strongest when if/types.h, concrete if*.h prototypes, HUD/status state structs, HSD_GObj unlink/setup patterns, and gm/mn caller signatures are updated together.
src/melee/if/** include/melee/if/**
The target path is under src/melee/if or a gm/mn caller touches HUD/name/time/prize/status code. Generated output walks interface GObjs, status arrays, timer/name data, or HSD_SisLib text objects.
Check if/types.h and the target if*.h before adding temporary HUD/status structures. Use explicit HSD_GObj and HSD_Text/SisLib types when prize, timer, name tag, stock, status, or text code proves them. When header prototypes change, inspect gm/mn callers because interface helpers are often scene-owned.
Leave NOT_IMPLEMENTED or UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in interface headers after a body proves the signature. Treat a small if file as isolated when game-mode or menu callsites consume the same state. Keep raw traversal helpers if direct control flow is the evidenced match for a near-matching status walk.

title: Path fact: Use item accessors, correct ItemVars union arms, special attributes, and item state tables
text:
Use item accessors, correct ItemVars union arms, special attributes, and item state tables
it
Item code commonly wants Item_GObj*, GET_ITEM(gobj), the correct Item_ItemVars union arm, explicit x4_specialAttributes typing, item state table/callback types, and header prototype cleanup when evidence supports the signature.
src/melee/it/** include/melee/it/**
The target function operates on an item GObj or Item data. Generated output accesses gobj->user_data, ip->xDD4_itemVar, x4_specialAttributes, item state tables, or item callback prototypes.
Use Item_GObj* and Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj) when header/caller evidence supports item user data. Select the correct ip->xDD4_itemVar.<item> union member; add or fix the union arm only with source/header/objdiff evidence. Cast or type xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes to the evidenced item attribute struct. Update item headers and item state callback return types when the function body proves them.
Leave raw gobj->user_data temporaries as finished source when GET_ITEM represents the operation. Use an unrelated ItemVars arm because m2c defaulted to it. Define new attribute fields without offset, sibling-source, header, data, or objdiff evidence. Treat item facts as stronger than current source, headers, assembly, or objdiff.

title: Path fact: Library targets often need archive/preload varargs, collision/dynamics typing, and helper-specific stack/data quirks
text:
Library targets often need archive/preload varargs, collision/dynamics typing, and helper-specific stack/data quirks
lb
Library code is broad but recurring: use lb/types.h for PreloadEntry, DynamicsData, collision structs, and GXColor-bearing layouts; use lbArchive/lbDvd/lbHeap prototypes for varargs and cache state; and check helper-specific matching notes before removing unusual but evidenced arguments or sqrt helpers.
src/melee/lb/** include/melee/lb/**
The target path is under src/melee/lb. Generated output touches lbArchive varargs, PreloadEntry, lbHeap/lbDvd state, collision helpers, DynamicsData, GXColor, lbMthp, or sqrt/frsqrte helpers.
Use lb/types.h and target lb*.h prototypes before adding local structs. For archive/preload code, preserve varargs pairs, public symbol names, and PreloadEntry sentinel fields when evidenced. For collision/dynamics/draw code, type Vec3, GXColor, HitCapsule/HurtCapsule, and DynamicsData paths before raw pointer math. For math/movie helpers, check local sqrtf_accurate, __frsqrte, Movieplayer, and rate-table helpers before substituting generic library calls.
Drop unused-looking varargs from OSReport/lbArchive/lbHeap paths without objdiff evidence. Assume all lb modules share one style; lb collision, DVD, heap, vector, mthp, and bgflash have distinct struct surfaces. Add fake data anchors when symbol metadata or static headers own local constants.

title: Path fact: Menu work benefits from typed menu state, SisLib text, cursor/user-data structs, and archive asset names
text:
Menu work benefits from typed menu state, SisLib text, cursor/user-data structs, and archive asset names
mn
Menu files usually become tractable when target-specific state structs, static headers, HSD_GObj user-data, HSD_SisLib/HSD_Text calls, and archive asset-name arrays are recovered together. gm/if/lb callsites often need matching prototype cleanup.
src/melee/mn/** include/melee/mn/**
The target path is under src/melee/mn. Generated output touches HSD_SisLib, HSD_Text, cursor state, static menu data, archive symbol names, controller navigation, or menu scene callsites.
Check mn/types.h, the target mn*.h, and any mn*.static.h before adding state fields. Use HSD_SisLib/HSD_Text names for rendered text and cursor/menu labels when calls prove them. Preserve archive public symbol names such as MenMain*, cursor, stage-select, and gallery asset strings as data/split evidence.
Leave broad UNK prototypes in menu headers after implementation proves HSD_GObj, HSD_Text, Vec3, bool, or u8 pointer types. Treat static data movement as harmless; menu data and BSS order often affect matching. Flatten menu object lifetimes without checking GObj unlink/setup and parent/child user-data paths.

title: Path fact: Map collision targets need mpColl/mpLib flag structs, diagnostic strings, line IDs, and GXColor draw helpers
text:
Map collision targets need mpColl/mpLib flag structs, diagnostic strings, line IDs, and GXColor draw helpers
mp
Map/collision code is easiest to review when mp/types.h and mplib/mpcoll headers define line/collision structs, env flags, diagnostic OSReport strings, and GXColor draw helpers rather than leaving anonymous arrays and raw offsets.
src/melee/mp/** include/melee/mp/**
The target path is under src/melee/mp or map collision is called by fighter, item, stage, or crowd logic. Generated output touches mpColl, mpLib line data, collision flags, JObj coll warnings, GXColor debug drawing, or map boundary globals.
Use mp/types.h, mplib.h, and mpcoll.h before adding temporary collision structs. Preserve diagnostic OSReport file/line strings as matching and semantic evidence. For debug draw paths, type GXColor constants and line/floor/ceiling/wall color helpers explicitly.
Treat map boundary globals as untyped floats if mpLib exposes a struct or named color/line helper. Remove diagnostic strings or file-line arguments just because generated output looks noisy. Change map collision helper signatures without checking item/fighter/stage callers.

title: Path fact: Player/bonus targets depend on slot-indexed stats, stale-move tables, and accurate math helpers
text:
Player/bonus targets depend on slot-indexed stats, stale-move tables, and accurate math helpers
pl
Player code often needs slot-indexed arrays, concrete bonus/stat/stale-move structs, truthful plbonus/plbonuslib prototypes, and the local accurate sqrt helper shape used for matching. Cross-check ft and gm callers when changing these fields.
src/melee/pl/** include/melee/pl/**
The target path is under src/melee/pl or fighter/game-mode code touches player bonus/stat data. Generated output accesses player slots, MatchPlayerData, stale move tables, plbonus thresholds, player archive data, or sqrt/frsqrte helper code.
Use pl/types.h, player.h, plbonus*.h, and plstale.h before adding raw pads. Prefer arrays such as kills[4] or stale-move tables when source/header evidence shows indexed data. Preserve my_sqrtf/__frsqrte-style helpers when local codegen evidence requires them.
Split player stats into scalar fields when array indexing is evidenced. Replace local accurate sqrt helpers with generic sqrtf without checking objdiff and .sdata2 effects. Change player/bonus structs without checking ft/gm callsites and header include paths.

title: Path fact: Crowd SFX code is small but config-heavy: use CrowdConfig, CrowdSFX state, and map boundary evidence
text:
Crowd SFX code is small but config-heavy: use CrowdConfig, CrowdSFX state, and map boundary evidence
sfx
Crowd sound code already exposes CrowdConfig and CrowdSFX_UnkStruct in crowdsfx.h. Use those names and the mpLib boundary source before adding new raw M2C_FIELD accesses or anonymous config fields.
src/melee/sfx/** include/melee/sfx/**
The target path is under src/melee/sfx. Generated output touches crowd thresholds, blastzone checks, kb magnitude, gCrowdConfig, un_804D7050 state, or mpLib boundary offsets.
Use gCrowdConfig and CrowdSFX_UnkStruct fields from crowdsfx.h before raw offsets. When crowd logic references map boundaries, check mpLib/mp types for named bounds before adding M2C_FIELD. Keep threshold names tied to crowd behavior such as cheer limit, gasp count, blastzone, and knockback thresholds.
Invent a second crowd state struct when crowdsfx.h owns the layout. Treat M2C_FIELD boundary accesses as finished source if mpLib exposes better names. Rename config fields semantically without evidence from behavior, callers, or map data.

title: Path fact: Toy/trophy targets use GET_TOY, trophy globals, archive asset names, and panel/list state structs
text:
Toy/trophy targets use GET_TOY, trophy globals, archive asset names, and panel/list state structs
ty
Toy/trophy code wants GET_TOY(gobj), Toy/TrophyData/ToyAnimState types, archive public asset strings, and target-specific tylist/tydisplay/tyfigupon state rather than anonymous user-data and raw archive lookups.
src/melee/ty/** include/melee/ty/**
The target path is under src/melee/ty or gm/mn scene code calls trophy/toy helpers. Generated output touches Toy user data, trophy unlock/list data, ToyDsp/ToyFigupon assets, HSD_Archive public symbols, HSD_Pad scans, or display panel state.
Use ty/inlines.h GET_TOY when user data is a Toy. Check toy.h and types.h for TrophyData, ToyAnimState, display/list/figupon state, and helper prototypes. Preserve ToyDsp/ToyFigupon/ToyList archive symbol strings as evidence for data and object setup.
Leave trophy display/list state as opaque void* when ty headers expose the layout. Move pad helpers into the wrong translation unit if doing so shifts existing matches. Invent semantic trophy names without evidence from archive strings, caller roles, or data-sheet/corpus support.

title: Path fact: VI/cutscene files repeatedly load CObj camera animations, run vi_RunCamera, and coordinate with gm/cm/lb
text:
VI/cutscene files repeatedly load CObj camera animations, run vi_RunCamera, and coordinate with gm/cm/lb
vi
VI scene files are small but repetitive: use vi.h inline camera callback helpers, GET_COBJ/HSD_CObj animation flow, lb_80013B14 camera descriptor setup, and cm Camera_80028B9C mode calls instead of hand-expanding each camera callback.
src/melee/vi/** include/melee/vi/**
The target path is under src/melee/vi or gm/cm code calls video/intro scene helpers. Generated output touches HSD_CObj, vi_RunCamera, camera callback priorities, character file-name tables, or lb_80013B14 camera descriptors.
Use vi_RunCamera and GET_COBJ when callback code matches the inline helper. Check vi*.h prototypes and cm camera APIs before adding local declarations. Preserve character/file-name tables and CObj animation descriptor ownership when linking vi objects.
Duplicate HSD_CObjSetCurrent/EraseScreen/EndCurrent callback blocks if vi_RunCamera is the evidenced source form. Treat vi camera descriptors as independent of cm/lb helpers. Change vi prototypes without checking gm and cm callers.
